result:
The pre-registration is complete and validated. Here's a summary of what this iteration designed:

## Experiment 31 — pivot to a never-tried design family

**The read of the plateau:** Experiments 26–30 attacked trunk capacity, coverage calibration, readout width, and expert routing — all reverted — and every one of them sat on top of the same output parameterization that has been in place since experiment 2: a flat table of 1,024 independent ~217 m cell templates, scored by a dot product against one holistic 560-d crop vector. Three signatures indict that substrate itself: the converged model's train and eval medians are *equal* (~600–800 m — it can't even fit visited locations), rural Prignitz beats all three self-similar metros (lookalike-cell confusion), and mean/median ≈ 2 everywhere (a heavy tail of long-range aliases).

**The change (one focused swap, `runs/pending_experiment.json`):** the template tables are replaced by a **learned neural map** — an 8-channel raster over the area at ~32 m/cell, sized from `meta.json` dimensions (no area constants) — scored by **sliding cross-correlation of a crop-derived 4×4×8 fingerprint kernel** whose taps each summarize 32 m of ground (OrienterNet-family matching, entirely in the weights, no imagery shipped). A position only scores high if the whole spatial arrangement matches; the scoring function is shared across all ~45k positions; overlapping crops write coherently into the same map cells; and the decode grid drops from 217 m to 32 m pitch. Everything kept survives: the exp-12 bright/dark experts become two kernel generators blended by the same gate (correlation is linear in the kernel), and the C4 vote, sharpened decode, confidence + calibration, fresh-draw sampler, and 24-epoch cosine schedule are byte-identical — Gaussian-CE σ stays exactly 1.5/32 in map units.

**De-risked before hand-off:** I probed the one engineering unknown (`runs/_probe_iter11_corr_export.py`) — `F.conv2d` with per-sample runtime kernels exports to ONNX opset 17 and runs in onnxruntime at **2.16–2.40 MB / ~4 ms** on Berlin- and Munich-sized rasters (≈2.8–3.1 MB with the real trunk), comfortably inside both frozen gates, with the untrained model decoding to map center (the usual bounded-downside floor). A fallback formulation (16 shifted matmuls + `do_constant_folding=False`) is specified in the brief in case the full model differs.

**Prediction:** 600–720 m worst-case median (10–25% below the 797.91 m champion) at roughly unchanged coverage, metros gaining more than Prignitz; named failure mode is residual ±45° heading mismatch smearing correlation peaks. Figure passes `figcheck`, and the implementation brief restates the CLI and ONNX contracts. The harness can take it from here.

tool_input.command:
grep -n "CROP_PX\|gsd\|width\|height" /workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/pipeline/common.py | head -20; echo ===; for a in berlin prignitz munich frankfurt; do python3 -c "
import json
m=json.load(open('/workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/data/areas/$a/meta.json'))
print('$a', m['width'], m['height'], m.get('gsd_m'))" 2>/dev/null || ls /workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/data/areas/ 2>/dev/null; done
tool_input.description: Check crop size and per-area raster dimensions

tool_input.command: for a in berlin prignitz munich frankfurt; do echo -n "$a: "; python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load(open('data/'+sys.argv[1]+'/meta.json')); print({k:m[k] for k in m if k in ('width','height','gsd_m')})" $a; done

python3 - <<'EOF'
import json
for a in ("berlin","prignitz","munich","frankfurt"):
    m = json.load(open(f"data/{a}/meta.json"))
    print(a, {k: m[k] for k in m if k in ("width","height","gsd_m")})
EOF

tool_input.description: Check training device, wall time, GPU
